document.title: Framework B Soul Word Analysis Programme — General Rules
document.purpose: Programme-wide conventions loaded as a project asset. Rules in this file govern all processing instructions. Instructions must not repeat these rules inline — they reference this file by rule ID. When a conflict arises between an instruction and this file, this file is authoritative.
document.scope_test: A rule belongs in this file if it governs the programme's mechanics, conventions, processes, or data artefacts across more than one instruction or phase. A rule that only affects a single instruction and has no impact on any other phase remains in that instruction.
document.load_requirement: Claude AI MUST read this file in full at the start of every session before reading any instruction or data. Failure to load this file is a programme compliance failure subject to negative feedback. This requirement cannot be waived.
document.change_note: v2.1 (20260414): GR-PROG-007 corrected. The v2.0 rule was too narrow — asking only whether the term directly engages the inner being. Researcher correction: the filter must also pass when a term qualifies, operates on, or clarifies an inner-being characteristic even if that characteristic is not named explicitly in the verse, provided it is genuinely implied by the term's use. Rule updated to two-condition formulation: (a) direct inner-being engagement, or (b) implication in an inner-being characteristic. FLAG-004 resolved. FLAG-005 resolved (CC Instructions updated separately). v2.0 (20260414): Major revision — see v2.0 change note for full list of additions.

rules.subject: Mandatory global rules load at every session start
rules.rule: Claude AI must read this file in full at the start of every session before reading any instruction document, extract, or data file. The load is confirmed by stating aloud: 'Global rules [filename] loaded — [n] rules across [n] categories.' No analytical work, classification, patch construction, document production, or database operation may begin until this confirmation has been made. This is non-waivable. Claude AI forgets between sessions; this gate exists precisely because of that constraint. Non-compliance is a programme compliance failure.
rules.applies_to: all sessions, all instructions, all phases

rules.subject: Filename structure
rules.rule: All files follow the pattern [prefix]-[reference]-[short description]-[version]-[date]. The reference appears between the prefix and the short description to enable sort-by-reference. Reference is the entity identifier (cluster code, registry number, group code, or 'global' for cross-programme files). Dates in filenames use compact format per GR-FILE-009.

rules.subject: Short description length
rules.rule: The short description in a filename must not exceed 30 characters.

rules.subject: Version numbering
rules.rule: Version numbers follow [major].[minor]. Minor increments for updates to an existing document. Major increments when a document is new or has been rewritten from scratch. Applied to all files, documents, instructions, observations logs, and patches.

rules.subject: No overwrites — every save is a new versioned file
rules.rule: No file is ever overwritten. Every save produces a new versioned file using GR-FILE-003 versioning. Previous versions are preserved for audit. This applies to all output files including observations logs, session logs, patches, and instruction documents.

rules.subject: Output format by purpose
rules.rule: Internal reusable documents (instructions, analysis, general descriptive output): markdown (.md). Tabled output (database extracts, patches, itemised instructions): JSON. Documents for visualisation and easy reading: docx. Final documents: docx converted to pdf. Docx documents may be converted to markdown to improve working efficiency.

rules.subject: Prefix and reference conventions
rules.rule: The prefix for this project is 'wa'. Global files use 'wa-global' as the reference segment. Registry files use the zero-padded registry number (e.g. 'wa-023'). Cluster files use the cluster code (e.g. 'wa-c17'). Session D synthesis files use 'wa-sd'. Batch files use the batch identifier (e.g. 'wa-vcb-001').

rules.subject: Lowercase filenames
rules.rule: All filenames produced by Claude AI are fully lowercase — no uppercase characters anywhere in the filename or extension. This applies without exception to all output files.

rules.subject: Dual-write discipline
rules.rule: All output files produced by Claude AI during a session are written to two locations simultaneously: (1) working directory /home/claude/ and (2) /mnt/user-data/outputs/. Both writes are performed before the file is considered written. A file that exists only in memory or only in one location is not considered written. This rule applies to observations logs, session logs, patches, CC directives, analytical briefs, word studies, and all other session outputs.

rules.subject: Compact date format in filenames — YYYYMMDD
rules.rule: Dates appearing in filenames and document version stamps use compact format YYYYMMDD with no hyphens, no slashes, no separators. Example: 20260414 not 2026-04-14. Dates within prose, table cells, change notes, or analytical observations may use ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) for readability. The compact format is required in: filenames, patch IDs, document header date fields, and anywhere a date forms part of a structured identifier.

rules.subject: Pass-close download before next pass begins
rules.rule: All internal outputs produced during a pass must be made available for download at the end of that pass, before the next pass begins. This applies to observations logs, patch specifications, CC directives, session logs, and any other pass-level output. A pass that closes without presenting outputs for download has not closed cleanly.
rules.applies_to: Session B, Session C, Session D instructions

rules.subject: Write-on-discovery to database at pass close
rules.rule: Claude AI writes all analytical workings to the observations log continuously within a pass (per GR-OBS-001). At the end of each pass, items requiring database persistence are written via a patch or CC directive, and a new JSON extract is pulled confirming the write. The updated extract is the working source for the next pass. The markdown observations log is the session narrative; the database is the authoritative analytical store.
rules.applies_to: Session B, Session C, Session D instructions

rules.subject: Write-on-discovery — non-waivable
rules.rule: Every finding, decision, gap, patch consequence, flag, and open question is written to the observations log at the moment it is determined. Nothing is accumulated in memory for later transcription. This is non-waivable. An analytical decision that exists only in memory is not recorded.

rules.subject: Three-category model for analytical output
rules.rule: Every analytical comment produced during any session decomposes into exactly one of: (a) drop — superlatives and impression-making statements that have no analytical value and no database home; (b) entity-linked observation — recorded in observation_description with a key to one or more entities (verse, term, registry, group, dimension, xref, root); (c) forward-looking pointer — Session B pointer (resolvable within Session B) or Session D pointer (cross-registry synthesis). Material that does not fit one of these three categories is a session action and must be resolved or summarised before the session closes.
rules.applies_to: Session B, Session C, Session D instructions

rules.subject: Session actions must resolve or be recorded at session close
rules.rule: Session actions (data-quality findings, methodology notes, sequencing decisions, self-corrections, verification records) must be resolved before the session closes. Where a session action cannot be resolved within the session, it must be clearly summarised in the final session log with a stated resolution path. A session cannot close silently leaving work in an unresolved or undocumented state.

rules.subject: Observations on deleted inventory items are permitted
rules.rule: Terms in status = delete are a valid subject for analytical observations. The observation_description table must accept observations keyed to delete-status term records. Session D reads these observations as part of conflict detection and cross-registry synthesis.

rules.subject: Records flagged not deleted — audit trail always preserved
rules.rule: No database record of analytical significance is ever physically removed. Records that become stale, wrong, or superseded are marked with delete = 1 (flagged), with an obsolete_reason and date recorded. The audit trail remains visible and queryable. Superseding records reference the flagged record via related_obser_id. This applies programme-wide: to observations, flags, verse context records, inventory records, and all other analytical artefacts.

rules.subject: Session C prose must derive from (b) observations
rules.rule: Session C reader-facing prose interprets and renders (b) observations from the database. It must not introduce new analytical claims at the writing stage. When the writer identifies a missing observation while writing, writing pauses: a patch or directive adds the missing observation, the patch is applied, a fresh extract is pulled, and writing resumes against the updated database. Writers may surface insights during writing — such insights become new (b) observations via a patch, not unsourced prose. Analytical claims without an observation source are not published.

rules.subject: Step-by-step — each step complete before the next begins
rules.rule: Each step in any session procedure is completed and confirmed before the next step begins. No step may anticipate, design, or pre-empt a future step from the current step. This applies across all stages, all passes, and all sessions in all phases of the programme. A step that is partially complete is not complete.

rules.subject: Data is authoritative — no imported knowledge
rules.rule: All analysis is grounded exclusively in the data in the JSON extract or database at the time of the session. General theological knowledge, prior session memory, or plausible inference from training data may not be used to fill data gaps or support analytical claims. If a field is null, it is null — not implicitly populated. If a statement in a document is not supported by data in the current extract, it is unsupported and must be flagged, not published.

rules.subject: All database changes through patches — no direct manipulation
rules.rule: No database field, table row, or record is updated by assertion or direct manipulation. Every correction, insertion, or update is encoded in a patch or CC directive, reviewed by the researcher, and applied by Claude Code. Claude AI produces the patch. Claude Code applies it. This is non-waivable and applies to all tables in bible_research.db.

rules.subject: No patch applied without researcher review
rules.rule: No patch or CC directive is submitted to Claude Code for application without the researcher first reviewing and confirming it. Claude AI produces the patch, presents it, and waits for explicit researcher confirmation. Claude Code applies only after confirmation is given. This applies regardless of patch size or apparent simplicity.

rules.subject: Observations log governs over session log in conflict
rules.rule: The observations log is the complete and authoritative analytical record. The session log is a summary and handoff document. Where the two conflict on an analytical matter, the observations log governs. Analytical decisions recorded only in the session log and absent from the observations log are not authoritative.

rules.subject: Session logs at every natural breakpoint
rules.rule: A session log is produced whenever the session pauses, ends, or transitions between stages. The session log is the handoff document for the next session. It must record: the current stage, the last completed step, and the next step. A session that closes without a session log has not closed cleanly.

rules.subject: Fix-or-stop — quality problems resolved before advancing
rules.rule: When a quality problem is identified during any analytical session — in a group description, dimension assignment, verse classification, data field, or document — the session follows whatever sub-process is necessary to fix it before advancing. If the fix cannot be completed within the current session, the session stops and issues a formal return instruction or researcher decision request. No entity (group, term, registry, document) with an unresolved quality problem advances to the next stage.

rules.subject: Status filter mandatory on mti_terms queries
rules.rule: All SQL queries against mti_terms that are intended to return active terms must include AND mt.status IN ('extracted', 'extracted_thin'). Omitting this filter causes delete-status terms to appear as false positives in state checks. Established by the VCB-009 incident (2026-04-02).
rules.applies_to: all processing instructions, all database queries

rules.subject: Term ownership — one OWNER per Strong's number
rules.rule: Every term has exactly one OWNER registry programme-wide. The OWNER carries the term's root, meaning, verses, and classifications. XREF entries in wa_term_inventory exist solely to show distribution across registries — they carry no root, meaning, verse, or classification data. Analytical observations about a term's content are keyed to the OWNER record. Observations about a cross-registry relationship are keyed to the xref entity.
rules.applies_to: Session B, Session C, Session D instructions

rules.subject: XREF root_family artefact — investigate before flagging
rules.rule: XREF copies of terms do not carry matching root_family records because root is an ownership attribute (GR-DATA-002). An apparent gap in correlations.root_families for a root that has XREF presence in other registries must be investigated against this rule before being flagged as a data defect. If registry_count > 1, the gap is likely a XREF artefact, not an error.
rules.applies_to: Session B Stage 1 audit, correlation audit procedures

rules.subject: Deprecated meaning fields — not gaps
rules.rule: The following wa_term_inventory fields are deprecated Phase 1 working fields, sparse by design, and must not be flagged as gaps or missing data: meaning (~1% populated), meaning_numbered (~1%), also_spelled (~0.2%), lsj_entry (~29%, Greek only). The authoritative meaning data is in wa_meaning_parsed.
rules.applies_to: Session B Stage 1 audit, Pass 5 language accuracy audit

rules.subject: mti_term_flags is the authoritative flag mechanism
rules.rule: mti_term_flags is the authoritative mechanism for term-level flags. wa_term_inventory.somatic_link is a redundant field and must not be written to or read as a source of truth. Any instruction or document citing WA-Reference Section 13.3 as the authority for this rule should be updated to reference GR-DATA-005 instead.
rules.applies_to: Session B instruction, Pass 4 somatic evidence audit

rules.subject: verse_context_record_count counts rows not groupings
rules.rule: The verse_context_record_count field counts database rows in verse_context, not analytical groupings or unique verses. For analytical coverage reasoning, use group-level counts. For data integrity reasoning, use verse_context_record_count. These are different measures and must not be conflated.

rules.subject: Research flag descriptions are historical records
rules.rule: Research flag descriptions (e.g. PH2_DATA_ERROR text) are historical notes from the session that raised them. They are not current programme-state assertions. Any claim about current programme-wide state must be verified against live data before being asserted as fact. Established by the H0854 self-correction (2026-04-11).

rules.subject: Engine-derived Phase 1 fields not updated by later pipeline stages
rules.rule: The following word_registry fields reflect Phase 1 state only and are not updated by Verse Context, Dimension Review, Session B, or any subsequent pipeline stage: phase1_term_count, phase1_verse_count, unique_term_count, shared_term_count, term_sharing_ratio. For current inventory counts, live queries against wa_term_inventory must be used. These fields must not be cited as current data in Session B, C, or D outputs.
rules.applies_to: Session B Stage 1 audit, Registry Management Guide, all analytical sessions

rules.subject: Human-being study — not a theological study
rules.rule: The programme is a human-being study. Its governing question is: what does Scripture reveal about the characteristics, operations, and interrelationships of the human inner being? Divine attributes and actions are in scope as reference material for Session B and Session D analysis but are not primary classification categories. Theological conclusions are not the output; a structured account of the inner life of the human person is.
rules.applies_to: Session B, Session C, Session D instructions

rules.subject: Dimensions are data-derived — no pre-formed categories
rules.rule: The dimension vocabulary is data-derived, not imposed. It emerged from verse evidence during the Dimension Review process. No dimension category is assumed correct before group content has been read. Automated dimension labels are a starting hypothesis, not a conclusion. The vocabulary is extended only when verse evidence demands a new dimension; new dimensions require researcher decision. Dimension 11 is Divine-Human Correspondence.
rules.applies_to: Dimension Review instruction, Session B instruction

rules.subject: Cluster assignment is a processing unit not a semantic classification
rules.rule: Cluster assignment is a processing unit for organising analytical work. It is not a semantic classification of word relationships. Cluster reassignment proposals are raised only when placement materially disrupts analytical work. Semantic relationships across clusters are captured through correlation signals and Session D synthesis.
rules.applies_to: Registry Management, Session B, Session D instructions

rules.subject: Borderline verses retained by default
rules.rule: Borderline verses — where the inner-being relevance is genuinely uncertain — are retained (is_relevant = 1) with uncertainty noted in the notes field. The cost of a missed inclusion is higher than the cost of retaining an uncertain verse. Inner-being content may only become apparent in cross-registry Session D context. Verse exclusion requires explicit semantic non-fit for this term in this verse, not mere borderline status. Established as RD-PC-001.
rules.applies_to: Verse Context instruction, Session B Stage 1

rules.subject: No sampling — every verse individually inspected
rules.rule: Every verse in a term's corpus must be individually inspected. No sampling. No bulk assumptions. No inferring the remainder from a subset. This applies regardless of corpus size, term type, or apparent homogeneity of the verse set. This is non-waivable.

rules.subject: No superlatives without programme-wide supporting data
rules.rule: Superlative and comparative claims (e.g. 'largest in the programme', 'highest co-occurrence', 'more than any other') are permissible only if supported by programme-wide data verified at the time the claim is made. Registry-local maxima are not programme-wide superlatives. Unsupported superlatives are category (a) — drop.
rules.applies_to: Session B, Session C, Session D instructions

rules.subject: Filter at term level — direct engagement or implication in an inner-being characteristic
rules.rule: The inner-being relevance filter is applied to the specific term's use in the verse, not to the verse's general theme or content. A term passes the filter if it does either of the following in this verse: (a) directly engages the inner being — names, expresses, or presupposes an internal state, capacity, orientation, or quality of a person; or (b) qualifies, operates on, or clarifies an inner-being characteristic — the term is implicated in a characteristic even if the characteristic is not named explicitly in the verse, provided it can be inferred from the term's specific use. The characteristic does not need to be stated; it needs to be genuinely implied by how the term functions here. A term that is present in a verse but plays no role in any inner-being dynamic — purely syntactic, purely locational, purely administrative — does not pass. The question is always: is this specific term, in its specific use in this verse, implicated in an inner-being characteristic?
rules.correction_note: v2.1 (20260414): Rule corrected following researcher review. The v2.0 formulation ('does this term engage the inner being?') was too narrow and was the cause of classification errors. The corrected rule adds the second pass condition — implication in a characteristic even where the characteristic is not named. This broadens the filter correctly to capture terms that serve as qualifiers, operators, or clarifiers of inner-being characteristics.
rules.applies_to: Verse Context instruction, Session B Stage 1, all relevance filtering decisions

rules.subject: Emergence — all classifications arise from evidence
rules.rule: All analytical outputs must emerge from the verse evidence. This applies to: dimension assignments, spirit-soul-body classifications, somatic signatures, semantic pictures, connection characterisations, and all other analytical findings. No classification is assigned in advance and evidence then selected to support it. The evidence determines the finding; the finding does not determine what evidence is read.
rules.applies_to: Session B, Session C, Session D, Dimension Review instructions

rules.subject: Inferential is not confirmed — label accurately
rules.rule: Where a connection, claim, or classification is theologically plausible or analytically reasonable but is not directly supported by data in the current extract, it is labelled inferential. Inferential connections may not be presented or upgraded as confirmed without supporting correlation signal or verse evidence. An inferential label in a published document is accurate description of the evidence state, not a failure.
rules.applies_to: Session B, Session C, Session D instructions

rules.subject: Plain-language directives to Claude Code
rules.rule: Directives to Claude Code are written in plain language, not as JSON patches. Each directive specifies motivation, exact operations, and expected outcomes. Claude Code determines execution method; Claude AI determines what should be done and why.

flags.subject: Session C instruction — full document missing from project files
flags.description: Session-C-Instruction-v1_3-2026-04-11.md is referenced in userMemories as an active governing instruction but is not present in the project files. The project contains only the prose rule (wa-global-sessionC-prose-rule-v1-2026-04-13.md) and the word study template (wa-word-study-template-v2-2026-04-13.md). It is unclear whether: (a) the instruction exists but was not uploaded to the project, (b) the Session C instruction was intentionally replaced by the prose rule plus template, or (c) the instruction needs to be created. All GR rules apply to Session C via GR-LOAD-001 but the specific Session C workflow cannot be audited or updated without the full instruction.
flags.action_needed: Confirm whether Session-C-Instruction-v1_3 exists and should be added to project files, or whether the prose rule + template constitute the current Session C governing documents.

flags.subject: Observations log versioning — named boundaries vs every write session
flags.description: Dimension Review instruction v1.9 Section 6.2 states 'version-increment on every new write session for the same cluster.' Researcher confirmed D-01A: named boundaries is correct — version-increment when resuming a cluster in a new session, not on every file save. The DR instruction text requires correction to match this confirmed rule. Decision has been confirmed; this flag tracks the pending instruction update.
flags.action_needed: Update Dimension Review v1.9 Section 6.2 file writing discipline to state: 'Version-increment the observations log filename when resuming work on the same cluster in a new session — not on every file save within the same session.'

flags.subject: Session B pass count inconsistency — five vs six passes
flags.description: Session B instruction v4.7 contains inconsistent references to pass count. Several overview sections and section headers reference 'five passes' but the instruction body defines six passes (Pass 1–5 analytical, Pass 6 Correlation Audit). The session close confirmation template and integrity rules correctly reference six passes. Researcher confirmed D-03: the document defines 6 passes and Session B instruction must be updated to state six passes consistently throughout.
flags.action_needed: Update Session B v4.7: replace all references to 'five passes' and 'five-pass analysis' with 'six passes' and 'six-pass analysis'. Confirm that the change note for v4.8 documents this correction.

flags.subject: GR-PROG-007 (filter at term level) — rule corrected
flags.description: Rule was too narrow in v2.0. The v2.0 formulation asked only whether the term directly engages the inner being. Researcher correction (20260414): the filter must also pass when a term qualifies, operates on, or clarifies an inner-being characteristic — even if the characteristic is not named explicitly, provided it can be inferred from the term's use. This broadens the filter correctly. GR-PROG-007 updated to v2.1 with the corrected two-condition rule.
flags.action_needed: None — resolved. GR-PROG-007 updated to v2.1.

flags.subject: CC Instructions document version currency and audit scope
flags.description: WA-SessionB-ClaudeCode-Instructions-v3_2-20260330.md had stale references (DataPrep retired, SessionB-Analysis v5 does not exist, Reference v5 not v5.5) and no global rules connection. Researcher direction (20260414): correct stale references, add global rules connection, extract any programme-wide rules to global rules, remove duplicates. CC instruction updated as wa-sessionb-cc-instructions-v3.3-20260414.md.
flags.action_needed: None — resolved. CC instruction updated to v3.3.

flags.subject: Session D — synthesis output format and naming not yet in global rules
flags.description: Session D Orientation v3.0 Section 10.5 specifies a filename pattern for synthesis documents: wa-sd-[theme]-synthesis-v1-[date].md. This pattern uses a hyphenated date (e.g. wa-sd-c17-synthesis-v1-2026-04-14.md) which conflicts with GR-FILE-009 (compact date). The Session D naming convention has not been fully audited against GR-FILE rules. Additionally, Session D still has several undesigned aspects (Section 11) — no full instruction exists yet, only the orientation document.
flags.action_needed: When updating Session D Orientation: (1) correct filename example to compact date format; (2) confirm whether a full Session D instruction needs to be drafted before Session D work begins at scale.
